item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved how the "tips" make sense once you read the reviews. A good mix of comedy and body horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems like the currency generation and the user reviews are two completely separate anomalies that you have tried to force into the same SCP. They might each be good on their own, but I don't think they work together. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kudos to you. I applaud you for having such great execution on a rather…Interesting idea. Execution was great. Writing was wonderful.

I especially loved how the 'disease' sorta progressed through the time, and went from making generate in people, to having them emit a WiFi signal. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this entry. Brilliant twist at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The end is the best part of the this skip to be honest. If you were to extend just by a little it could answer some of my questions and hopefully raise a thousand more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you're missing it. While I admit to also griping about unrelated things shoehorned into the end of SCPs, I would argue that the presence of user reviews and online connectivity is absolutely consistent with the 'disease', at least conceptually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Novote from me as well. Unless I'm missing something, the addendum, while amusing, seemed to exist purely to shoehorn a gimmick into an otherwise mundane skip. I can't draw any connection between the original effects of the disease and a WiFi signal, and so to me, that could have been added to just about any file on the site to similar effect.

(That being said, I've done very poorly so far with understanding SCPs that hide their explanations within subtle clues and hints, so if there is something to miss, I'm probably missing it.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's uber for organs and the title is just a bad pun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really have much else to say about this - the aim here was to let the idea speak for itself as ~biting social commentary~

If it doesn't work for you, fair enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed with this, but moving for a downvote. It's got a nice twist there but doesn't really build on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not quite sure what's going on here. And isn't "Übermensch" German for "Superman"? Novote, although the reviews made me laugh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just so fucking weird. Took a minute to click what was going on at the end there, but I love it. +1 from me. My only issue is that the second half of the description gets a little medical-jargony for my tastes, which kind of slowed things down a bit. Otherwise, I'm a sucker for the "internet reviews of weird bullshit" genre. Good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a pretty odd mishmash of ideas, but it works together decently at best. I'll give it a +1 for the excellent writing and cool idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was really into it at first, but right when it starts to get good it ends. If you add more then I’ll upvote, but for now it’s a no vote from me

item.title: 'Übermensch'
item.content:encoded:
The sharing economy is weird. I had this idea and really wanted to hammer it out.

Sources for images used in the final image: 1, 2
